returns._0: True if the feature is enabled, false otherwise.

upgrade((address,bytes32,bytes32)[]).details: This function is intended to be DELEGATECALLed by an address that is the common owner of every chain in      `_opChainConfigs`'s ProxyAdmin.This function requires that each chain's superchainConfig is already upgraded.

params._opChainConfigs: Array of OpChain structs, one per chain to upgrade

upgradeSuperchainConfig(address).details: This function is intended to be DELEGATECALLed by the superchainConfig's ProxyAdminOwner.This function will revert if the SuperchainConfig is already at or above the target version.

blueprints().notice: Retrieves the blueprint addresses stored in this OPCM contract

chainIdToBatchInboxAddress(uint256).notice: Maps an L2 chain ID to an L1 batch inbox address as defined by the standard configuration's convention. This convention is `versionByte || keccak256(bytes32(chainId))[:19]`, where || denotes concatenation`, versionByte is 0x00, and chainId is a uint256. https://specs.optimism.io/protocol/configurability.html#consensus-parameters

devFeatureBitmap().notice: Retrieves the development feature bitmap stored in this OPCM contract

implementations().notice: Retrieves the implementation addresses stored in this OPCM contract

isDevFeatureEnabled(bytes32).notice: Retrieves the status of a development feature. Note that this function does not check         that the input feature represents a single feature and the bitwise AND operation         allows for multiple features to be enabled at once. Users should generally check         for only a single feature at a time.

upgrade((address,bytes32,bytes32)[]).notice: Upgrades a set of chains to the latest implementation contracts

blueprints().notice: Retrieves the blueprint addresses stored in this OPCM contract

chainIdToBatchInboxAddress(uint256).notice: Maps an L2 chain ID to an L1 batch inbox address as defined by the standard configuration's convention. This convention is `versionByte || keccak256(bytes32(chainId))[:19]`, where || denotes concatenation`, versionByte is 0x00, and chainId is a uint256. https://specs.optimism.io/protocol/configurability.html#consensus-parameters

devFeatureBitmap().notice: Retrieves the development feature bitmap stored in this OPCM contract

implementations().notice: Retrieves the implementation addresses stored in this OPCM contract

isDevFeatureEnabled(bytes32).notice: Retrieves the status of a development feature. Note that this function does not check         that the input feature represents a single feature and the bitwise AND operation         allows for multiple features to be enabled at once. Users should generally check         for only a single feature at a time.

upgrade((address,bytes32,bytes32)[]).notice: Upgrades a set of chains to the latest implementation contracts

Upgraded(uint256,address,address).notice: Emitted when a chain is upgraded

AddressHasNoCode(address).notice: Throw when a contract address has no code.

BytesArrayTooLong().notice: Thrown when converting a bytes array to a uint256 and the bytes array is too long.

EmptyInitcode().notice: Thrown when parsing a blueprint preamble and the resulting initcode is empty.

IdentityPrecompileCallFailed().notice: Thrown when call to the identity precompile fails.

InvalidGameArgsLength().notice: Thrown when the length of the game arguments is invalid.

NotABlueprint().notice: Thrown when parsing a blueprint preamble and the bytecode does not contain the expected prefix bytes.

OPContractsManagerUpgrader_SuperchainCon.notice: Thrown when upgradeSuperchainConfig is called with a superchainConfig that is already up to date.
Thrown when the SuperchainConfig contract does not match the unified config.
Thrown when upgrade is called with a chain whose superchainConfig is not upgraded.

OPContractsManager_InvalidGameType().notice: Thrown when an invalid game type is used.

PrestateNotSet().notice: Thrown when a prestate is not set for a game.

ReservedBitsSet().notice: Thrown when parsing a blueprint preamble and the reserved bits are set.

SemverComp_InvalidSemverParts().notice: Error thrown when a semver string has less than 3 parts.

UnexpectedPreambleData(bytes).notice: Thrown when parsing a blueprint preamble and the preamble data is not empty. We do not use the preamble data, so it's expected to be empty.

UnsupportedERCVersion(uint8).notice: Thrown during deployment if the ERC version is not supported.

returns._0: True if the feature is enabled, false otherwise.

upgrade((address,bytes32,bytes32)[]).details: This function is intended to be DELEGATECALLed by an address that is the common owner of every chain in      `_opChainConfigs`'s ProxyAdmin.This function requires that each chain's superchainConfig is already upgraded.

params._opChainConfigs: Array of OpChain structs, one per chain to upgrade

upgradeSuperchainConfig(address).details: This function is intended to be DELEGATECALLed by the superchainConfig's ProxyAdminOwner.This function will revert if the SuperchainConfig is already at or above the target version.

params.systemConfig: Address of the chain's SystemConfig contract

@notice Helper method for computing a salt that's used in CREATE2 deployments.
 Including the contract name ensures that the resultant address from CREATE2 is unique
 across our smart contract system. For example, we deploy multiple proxy contracts
 with the same bytecode from this contract, so they each require a unique salt for determinism.

documentation.text:
@notice addGameType deploys a new dispute game and links it to the DisputeGameFactory. The inputted _gameConfigs
 must be added in ascending GameType order.

@notice Updates the prestate hash for dispute games while keeping all other parameters the same
 @param _prestateUpdateInputs The new prestate hashes to use

@notice Migrates the Optimism contracts to the latest version.
 @param _input Input parameters for the migration.

@notice Maps an L2 chain ID to an L1 batch inbox address as defined by the standard
 configuration's convention. This convention is `versionByte || keccak256(bytes32(chainId))[:19]`,
 where || denotes concatenation`, versionByte is 0x00, and chainId is a uint256.
 https://specs.optimism.io/protocol/configurability.html#consensus-parameters
